title: Policy Framework for Salmonella Testing in School Feeding Programs
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: School     feeding     programs     play     a     crucial     role   in   supporting     child     nutrition     and     learning     outcomes  ,   yet     they     can     also     be     a     potential     vehicle     for     foodborne     illnesses     if   not   properly     monitored  .   Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     pathogens     causing     foodborne     disease     outbreaks     worldwide  ,   particularly   in   vulnerable     populations     such   as   school-aged     children  .   Implementing     a     clear     policy     framework     for     routine     Salmonella   testing   within     school     feeding     programs     is     essential     to     prevent     illness  ,   minimize     absenteeism  ,   and     maintain     public     trust  .   This     framework     should     include     risk-based   sampling,   standardized     laboratory     methods  ,   and     transparent     reporting     systems  .   Coordinated     efforts     among     government     agencies  ,   schools  ,   and     food     suppliers     will     strengthen     food     safety     governance     and     ensure     the   long-term   sustainability     of     national     school     feeding     initiatives  .
